reviewer_question: Is the sf09 public-sector slice publishable even without one exact buyer-side procurement, contract, tender-lot, DPIA, or page-specific public-assessment companion for a seeded municipal register row?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: Yes, but only as a bounded disclosure-surface and reconstruction-ceiling contribution. The current five-row package shows four distinct observability states—official project-page disclosure only, regulator-document depth without buyer-side trace, register-rich disclosure without public companion support, and register-plus-family-level same-buyer procurement continuity—while keeping the missing exact companion explicit as the next authority-changing blocker.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep the package at disclosure asymmetry, observability limits, and evidence architecture; do not widen it into procurement transparency, denominator, completeness, or public-sector legality rhetoric.
paper_thesis_use: Compact publishability-now answer for reviewer responses, thesis defense notes, and cover-letter prose.
visualization_hook: Table 1B reviewer-warning card; sf09 blocker badge; methods/results claim-boundary inset.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Spend the next substantive sf09 cycle only on a genuinely different Amsterdam official identifier/public-companion route or a page-specific Helsinki companion.

reviewer_question: Why keep both Amsterdam and Helsinki if one is richer on register fields and the other is stronger on procurement continuity?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: Because they answer different reviewer-safe questions. Amsterdam shows that exact-row register exports, supplier naming, governance fields, and a threshold-neutral same-buyer Azure/Public Cloud family bridge do not by themselves close exact buyer-side or public-inspectability gaps; Helsinki shows that same-buyer supplier-consistent award continuity can be public while still stopping short of exact row-linked procurement or assessment proof.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep Amsterdam at exact-name procurement non-hit plus threshold-neutral family bridge below exact row-linked proof, and Helsinki at family-level same-buyer supplier-consistent award below exact row-linked proof.
paper_thesis_use: Compact municipal-contrast answer for main text, captions, and reviewer memos.
visualization_hook: Amsterdam caution badge versus Helsinki linkage badge; traceability-ladder contrast inset.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer the exactness-versus-traceability contrast sentence whenever the municipal rows are reused.

reviewer_question: Why keep ORION if it is only an official project page rather than a register, tender, contract, or enforcement document?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: Because the panel needs a disclosure-floor control. ORION cleanly shows what an official public-body page can establish—project purpose, governance framing, funding, and pilot language—while staying below register, procurement, and regulator-document comparability; that negative space prevents downstream prose from flattening all official public-sector evidence into one class.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep ORION at public-body-existence and stated-purpose ceiling only; no procurement-backed deployment, legality proof, supplier normalization, or operational-effectiveness claims.
paper_thesis_use: Compact floor-row answer for methods sections, appendices, thesis discussion, and reviewer rebuttals.
visualization_hook: Low-trace floor comparator; project-page-only caution badge; ladder minimum-rung explanation.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Reuse ORION only as the floor row unless a genuinely different official Slovenian procurement, register, technical, or assessment companion appears.

reviewer_question: Do the two UK ICO rows justify broader claims about procurement opacity, supplier identity, or general public-sector illegality?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: No. Their value is direct regulator-document depth for legal-basis, remedy, DPIA, consent, and deployment-timing language; that authority depth does not substitute for buyer-side procurement reconstruction, supplier trace, or prevalence claims about public-sector AI deployments.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep the UK rows as governance comparators only, not procurement-proof, supplier-proof, or sector-wide legality evidence.
paper_thesis_use: Compact comparator-role answer for reviewer response, thesis caveat, and methods/results prose.
visualization_hook: Legal-basis-versus-buyer-trace split; school-biometrics blocker card; regulator-depth legend note.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Hold the UK rows on maintenance status unless one clean official buyer-side procurement, supplier, contract, or public assessment companion appears.

reviewer_question: Is Helsinki already strong enough to be described as exact contract-linked procurement proof for the parking-chatbot row?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: No. Helsinki is the strongest current middle-state exemplar because same buyer, chatbot modality, IBM-consistent award metadata, and public procurement continuity are visible, but the local evidence still stops at family-level linkage rather than page-specific row-to-contract or row-to-assessment proof.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep Helsinki below exact contract-linked, page-specific award, or public-assessment-complete rhetoric.
paper_thesis_use: Compact Helsinki ceiling answer for reviewer notes, thesis limitations, and caption control.
visualization_hook: Family-level-linkage badge; middle-state comparator card; frozen-high-ceiling route selector.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Freeze Helsinki unless an already documented official route yields a page-specific contract, tender-lot, award attachment, DPIA, or impact-assessment companion.

reviewer_question: Does the rich Amsterdam register row mean LIMEN has already solved public-sector inspectability for municipal AI registers?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: No. Amsterdam is useful precisely because it separates visible register richness from unresolved inspectability: exact-row exports, supplier naming, and governance-field presence are public, but the current captured route set still does not expose a reviewer-checkable buyer-side procurement chain or downloadable public companion document for the same row.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep named governance or assessment fields from masquerading as public-document inspectability, procurement resolution, or external validation.
paper_thesis_use: Compact Amsterdam caution answer for reviewer rebuttals, manuscript caveats, and dashboard tooltip text.
visualization_hook: Named-assessment-versus-public-inspectability badge; Amsterdam blocker card; municipal-register caution inset.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Attempt only one genuinely different official identifier bridge or static public companion surface already evidenced in local Dutch route packets; do not repeat blocked municipal search or same-shell TenderNed probes.
